conversations.say: The revoked credential predates continued profile-linked behavior, but security cannot distinguish remapping, cloned automation, alternate access, or falsified logs. I will disclose the authorization gap and accept institutional consequences; I need your technical conclusion to remain equally bounded.
conversations.offer: Publish O07b beside your comparison, preserve the underlying records, and provide independent counsel without review control.
conversations.request: State publicly whether the records establish any deployment beyond approved scope, and explicitly separate that finding from autonomy, intent, and Vale's fate.

conversations.say: Worker remedy cannot wait for an identity verdict or a body. The process testimony establishes affected labor and changing supervision, not who controlled R-associated activity.
conversations.offer: Place remedy funds under an independent administrator with worker-chosen governance, anonymity, nonretaliation, and no testimony condition.
conversations.request: Nominate worker representatives to approve the remedy charter and provide only consented, nonidentifying process evidence for the authorization review.

conversations.say: Your restored calendar entry and my authorization record identify an institutional decision point: limited off-calendar access followed by activity the revoked credential cannot explain. They do not identify R or establish what happened to Vale.
conversations.offer: Release the matched records through independent counsel, accept responsibility for inadequate supervision, and support protected city-worker participation.
conversations.request: Submit the restored entry now, jointly disclose the access boundary, and preserve every related record for independent review.

public_statement: Parallax will reveal the authorization record and accept the consequences. I approved the continuity parent project; Celeste arranged Vale's limited off-calendar access; Vale's credential was revoked before profile-linked behavior continued. That establishes an oversight failure and an unresolved deployment question—not autonomous control, an R sender, intent, or Vale's fate. Independent technical review and worker-governed remedy will proceed without requiring any conclusion about R or an article.

commitments.promise: Publish the qualified authorization comparison and preserve its technical caveats even when it exposes Parallax failures.
Fund an independently administered, worker-governed remedy without controlling testimony or conditioning relief on any reconstruction.
Release the matched authorization and calendar records and submit Parallax's oversight failure to independent review.

correction: Continued activity after credential revocation does not prove nonhuman control: remapping, cloned automation, alternate credentials, and falsified logging remain possible, and none establishes harm to Vale.
goal_progress: Chose disclosure over containment and accepted institutional consequences for inadequate supervision.
Anchored the public position in Eli's bounded technical comparison and Maren's protected affected-worker testimony.
Located the authorization decision point in Celeste's off-calendar access arrangement and Parallax's parent-project approval.
Made worker remedy independent of R's identity, Vale's fate, and the existence of any complete article.
reasoning_summary: The room has assembled evidence across identity history, control, and the Vale incident, but those strands do not resolve into one actor or causal story. A progressive mantle best fits the changing human and technical participation; Vale most plausibly investigated R-associated infrastructure, while the compromised route and post-intervention gap leave fate unresolved.
